sense.definition: a system in cars that sends power to all four wheels at the same time, improving how well the vehicle grips the road

examples.x: On icy roads, all-wheel drive minimizes wheel spin and enhances acceleration by using all four wheels.
Drivers favor all-wheel drive for its versatility, handling city streets and rural roads alike.
The all-wheel drive engages automatically when it detects slipping or low traction, redistributing power to ensure stability.
Cars equipped with an all-wheel drive system offer enhanced performance and confidence in diverse driving conditions.
Manufacturers often highlight the benefits of their all-wheel drives, emphasizing improved safety and control for drivers.

sense.definition: a type of drivetrain that sends power to all four wheels, often used for driving on rough roads or off-road conditions

examples.x: A four-by-four system helps vehicles drive better on difficult roads by sending power to all four wheels instead of just two.
When driving through mud or snow, a four-by-four provides better traction and stability compared to cars with only front-wheel or rear-wheel drive.
Many trucks and SUVs use a four-by-four system to handle rough terrain and off-road adventures with confidence.
Drivers can switch between two-wheel drive and four-by-four mode to adapt to different driving conditions, such as highways or unpaved paths.
The four-by-four is popular among outdoor enthusiasts for its ability to navigate challenging landscapes and ensure safe travel in diverse weather conditions.

sense.definition: a mechanism that distributes the power received from the engine between four wheels instead of two
a system where power is transmitted to either the front or rear wheels for propulsion and steering control

examples.x: Front-wheel drive is a common type of two-wheel drive found in many compact cars, offering efficient traction and space utilization.
Two-wheel drive vehicles are generally lighter and more fuel-efficient compared to all-wheel drive or four-wheel drive options.
Manufacturers offer two-wheel drive across various vehicle types to cater to different driving preferences and cost considerations.
Classic muscle cars often featured rear-wheel drive, showcasing the raw power and drifting prowess of this two-wheel drive setup.
Many compact SUVs offer front-wheel drive as a two-wheel drive option, prioritizing affordability and fuel efficiency for urban driving conditions.

sense.definition: a system where the engine's power is primarily transmitted to the front wheels for propulsion, steering, and control

examples.x: In many smaller cars, the front-wheel drive setup allows for better fuel efficiency.
Front-wheel drive is generally considered safer in slippery conditions like snow or rain.
Most compact hatchbacks and sedans use front-wheel drive because it's more affordable to manufacture.
Front-wheel drive cars usually have a more spacious interior compared to rear-wheel drive models.

sense.definition: a system in a vehicle where the engine's power is sent to the rear wheels for movement

examples.x: Many sports cars use rear-wheel drive because it offers better handling and balance during high-speed turns.
In rear-wheel drive cars, the front wheels are primarily responsible for steering while the rear wheels provide the power.
Drivers often prefer rear-wheel drive in snowy conditions because the weight of the engine over the driving wheels improves traction.
Maintaining a rear-wheel drive vehicle can sometimes be more expensive due to the complexity of the drivetrain compared to front-wheel drive cars.
Some classic cars are famous for their rear-wheel drive setups, which enthusiasts appreciate for their driving dynamics and historical significance.

sense.definition: the system in a vehicle that ignites the fuel-air mixture in the engine's cylinders

examples.x: The ignition system failed, causing the engine to stall.
He upgraded the ignition system to improve fuel efficiency.
The ignition system included a keyless start feature for convenience.
They diagnosed a problem with the ignition system using diagnostic tools.
She checked the spark plugs as part of routine maintenance for the ignition system.

sense.definition: a system that assists the driver in steering the vehicle by reducing the effort needed to turn the steering wheel

examples.x: The power steering system made parking in tight spaces effortless.
She felt the difference in steering effort after the power steering was repaired.
He preferred cars equipped with power steering for easier maneuverability.
The power steering pump needed replacement due to a leak.
They adjusted the power steering fluid level to ensure smooth operation.

sense.definition: the system of springs, shock absorbers, and linkages that connect a vehicle to its wheels, providing a smooth ride and handling

examples.x: The suspension absorbed bumps in the road effectively.
She felt the car's suspension was too stiff for comfortable long-distance driving.
They tested the suspension components for wear and tear.
He upgraded the suspension with adjustable coilovers for better performance.

sense.definition: a safety feature in vehicles that prevents the wheels from locking up during sudden braking, helping to maintain steering control

examples.x: The car's anti-lock braking system helped prevent skidding when I had to stop suddenly.
My friend’s car has an anti-lock braking system, which makes driving in the rain much safer.
I feel much more confident driving in snowy conditions because my car has an anti-lock braking system.

sense.definition: a type of transmission that automatically changes gears based on vehicle speed, engine revolutions per minute, and throttle position

examples.x: She preferred driving automatic transmission vehicles in heavy traffic.
He shifted the automatic transmission to "Park" before turning off the engine.
The automatic transmission smoothly shifted gears during acceleration.
They flushed and refilled the automatic transmission fluid as part of maintenance.
The automatic transmission had a manual mode for more control over gear selection.

sense.definition: the system in a vehicle that removes exhaust gases from the engine and reduces noise

examples.x: The exhaust system emitted a loud noise when it developed a leak.
She noticed a decrease in engine performance due to a clogged exhaust system.
They inspected the exhaust system for rust and corrosion.
He upgraded the exhaust system with a high-flow catalytic converter.
The exhaust system included dual tailpipes for a sporty appearance.

sense.definition: a device in a motor vehicle used to maintain a constant speed that is based on the preference of the driver

examples.x: He set the cruise control to 65 miles per hour so he could relax his foot on the highway.
The long road trip was easier with cruise control keeping the car at a steady speed.
Using cruise control helped him maintain a consistent speed and save fuel.
She only uses cruise control on highways, not on city streets.

sense.definition: a system that controls the temperature and humidity in a house, car, etc.

examples.x: They had to call a technician to fix the broken air conditioning unit.
She enjoys the refreshing breeze provided by the air conditioning after a long day outside.
The air conditioning in the car was a lifesaver during the long road trip.

sense.definition: a system in a car that adjusts the interior temperature automatically based on preset preferences and current conditions

examples.x: My new car has automatic climate control, so I never have to worry about adjusting the temperature while driving.
With automatic climate control, you can set the desired temperature and the car will maintain it throughout the journey.
The automatic climate control in modern vehicles uses sensors to detect changes in temperature and adjust accordingly.
I find automatic climate control very convenient, especially during long trips when weather conditions can change.
Drivers appreciate automatic climate control because it allows them to focus more on the road without distractions.

sense.definition: a system in cars that helps drivers apply maximum braking force during sudden stops to prevent accidents

examples.x: My new car has emergency brake assist, which automatically increases braking power when it senses an impending collision.
The emergency brake assist kicked in when the driver ahead suddenly stopped, allowing us to avoid a rear-end collision.
Drivers should always be aware that emergency brake assist is not a substitute for careful driving and attentiveness on the road.
During the driving test, I demonstrated how emergency brake assist can aid in stopping the car quickly in unexpected situations.
Car manufacturers continually improve emergency brake assist systems to enhance safety and responsiveness on the roads.

sense.definition: a system in vehicles that helps prevent wheels from slipping on slippery surfaces

examples.x: My new car has traction control, so I feel safer driving in rainy weather.
The traction control kicked in when I accelerated too quickly on the icy road.
Drivers often appreciate traction control because it improves stability during turns.
If you're driving in mud or snow, traction control can make a big difference in keeping you on course.
Mechanics recommend keeping the traction control system well-maintained to ensure it works properly in all conditions.

sense.definition: a safety feature in cars that helps drivers maintain control during sudden maneuvers by automatically applying brakes to individual wheels

examples.x: When driving on slippery roads, electronic stability control can prevent skidding by adjusting the car's braking and steering.
Many modern vehicles come equipped with electronic stability control to enhance driver safety in various road conditions.
The main function of electronic stability control is to assist drivers in maintaining stability and direction when swerving or turning sharply.
Drivers should always check if their vehicle includes electronic stability control, as it significantly improves handling and reduces the risk of accidents.
Electronic stability control monitors the vehicle's movement and can intervene to prevent oversteering or understeering.

sense.definition: a feature in cars that alerts drivers when their vehicle unintentionally drifts out of its lane

examples.x: Lane departure warning systems are designed to enhance driving safety by notifying drivers if they start to veer out of their lane without signaling.
When activated, the lane departure warning system emits a gentle beep or vibration to warn the driver of potential lane drift.
My new car comes with advanced safety features, including a lane departure warning system that helps me stay focused on the road.
Drivers who frequently travel long distances appreciate lane departure warning systems because they reduce the risk of accidents caused by momentary lapses in attention.
Although helpful, lane departure warning systems are not a substitute for attentive driving and should be used in conjunction with safe driving practices.

sense.definition: the system in a vehicle that monitors its performance and detects potential issues

examples.x: Modern cars use on-board diagnostics to check if there are any problems with the engine or other systems.

sense.definition: a system in modern vehicles that injects fuel into the engine cylinders under high pressure for efficient combustion

examples.x: She cleaned the fuel injectors to maintain the fuel injection system's performance.
They diagnosed a fuel injector malfunction in the fuel injection system.
The fuel injection system improved fuel efficiency compared to carburetors.
The fuel injection system adjusted fuel delivery based on engine load and speed.
He upgraded the fuel injection system with direct injection technology.

sense.definition: a system in a vehicle that enables all doors to be locked or unlocked simultaneously using a single switch or key

examples.x: My new car has central locking, so I can lock all the doors with just one press of a button.
Central locking makes it convenient to secure your vehicle quickly when you're in a hurry.
Drivers appreciate central locking because it enhances safety by ensuring all doors are securely locked while driving.
When parking in unfamiliar areas, central locking gives me peace of mind knowing that my car is securely locked.
The repair shop fixed the central locking system in my car, so now I can easily unlock it from any door handle.

sense.definition: a technology that allows a person to unlock and lock their car doors from a distance using a small electronic device

examples.x: Many modern cars come equipped with a remote keyless system that makes it convenient to access the vehicle without inserting a physical key.
To use the remote keyless system, simply press the button on the remote control to unlock the car doors from several meters away.
Drivers appreciate the remote keyless system during rainy days because they can unlock the car without getting wet.
Some remote keyless systems also allow users to start the car's engine remotely, which is helpful in extreme weather conditions.
It's important to keep the remote keyless system's battery charged to ensure it works properly when needed.

sense.definition: a small electronic device that remotely controls functions such as locking and unlocking doors on a car

examples.x: My new car came with a key fob that allows me to unlock the doors from a distance.
The key fob also has a panic button in case of emergencies.
I often forget where I put my key fob because it's so small.
She pressed the button on her key fob to open the trunk of her car.
He replaced the battery in his key fob when it stopped working properly.

sense.definition: a satellite system that shows a place, thing, or person's exact position using signals
a device that uses GPS technology and satellite signals to help drivers navigate to their destination by providing them with real-time information about their location and route

examples.x: Satellite navigation systems are especially useful when traveling in unfamiliar areas.
He disconnected the satellite navigation after arriving at his destination.

sense.definition: a microprocessor-based device that manages and controls electrical systems within vehicles, ensuring optimal performance and functionality

examples.x: The electronic control unit also controls climate settings and entertainment systems in many vehicles today.
